item.title: Idk if we can come back from this
item.description: Man of all the things to get to FP for... Also to clarify, I get that many feel that things got worse when people started spamming politics stuff, which I do agree with, but when the company that bought this place is at the point of scrapping it for parts it feels like the days of this website are numbered
item.dc:creator: jypaof

item.title: Hit 'em where it hurts
item.dc:creator: thrane88

item.title: The Online Trans Pipeline is Real
item.description:
From straight bro… to femboy… to trans influencer. The Online Trans Pipeline is real and monetizable - I give you the full breakdown in my new YouTube video. https://youtu.be/tdLYKaFCA8I For those wondering how I can post in these trying times - I am not ignoring what is going on here. I have made my stance pretty clear on BlueSky how I feel if you want a more complete breakdown, and part of why I am pushing my YouTube today is because I plan on dropping a video in the following weeks specifically about Imgur and this entire situation to get the word out to more people - also trying to bring on a guest speaker if I can manage it.
The easiest way to put it: Imgur and my marriage is certainly on the rocks.
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.title: ATTENTION ALL THIRD PARTY DATA COLLECTORS: I have EXACTLY what you're looking for!
item.description:
Dear third party data collecting companies, Stop sweating about how to target your desired demographic, and let Medialab do all the heavy lifting for you!
Medialab reaches 180 MILLION GLOBAL USERS!! You would be STUPID not to jump on this unique opportunity!
Medialab has access to data collected from the very hugely popular social media website *checks notes* "Imger?" This platform has 41 MILLION US monthly unique visitors. I shit you not. These numbers don't lie. They came straight from the source! --> https://medialab.la/brands
With Medialab, you can reach 90 MILLION followers WHERE THEY ARE!! Plus, you can "Rich?" FIRST-PARTY DATA across their portfolio. Now, you can stay current on trends and ahead of what's happening next!!
Trust me, bro. This shit works. They have Case Studies:
THEY ARE IN BIG KETCHUP'S POCKET! YOU CAN BE TOO!! THIS community RIGHT HERE ALONE helped Heinz become a part of everyday and holiday moment. Remember that time the Imgur community participated in the "organic" Halloween Costume Contest? Each one of those TotallyFakeInternetPoints™ was BIG MONEY in their pockets!! BUT HOMIE, YOU HAVE POCKETS TO FILL TOO!! GET IN ON THIS SHIT!!
They have ALL the lyrics to all of your targeted audience's favorite songs, so you can easily stay on top of trending music to keep your advertisements HIP and FRESH.
Community driven ❤️
You can have a 10% increase in brand familiarity!!
YOU CAN SMELL LIKE THIS GUY
Ooooor you can deliver 9 MILLION whole ass impressions.
Maybe you're not a third-party data collector, but you are still very intrigued, well fuckin GUESS WHAT. You can work for them!! Medialab is set to DOUBLE IN SIZE every year for the next FOUR [understood noun]
Check out these other widely known, super popular, and thriving social media sites they have their hands on!
Medialab also cares about your privacy.
They respect your privacy by only collecting absolutely necessary information "most" times.
They only need to collect information that is critical to proper website functionality, such as, but not limited to: your name, address, email, and phone number. This way, if the website were to crash for any reason, Medialab can still provide you with your favorite meme content via email, text, snail mail, and even a fucking phone call. They are DEDICATED to satisfying YOUR targeted needs 24/7.
They also collect information that can help hook up your lame ass with some fuckin people you may know so you can go outside together and touch grass, you fuckin nerds.
They also have to keep any credit card information or payment account information. They're also gonna need to hold onto your authentication information. They would never sell out that vulnerable information though. Rest assured.
You never have to worry about being lost again! Medialab will collect and use your location information both while you are directly using the services AND in the 'BACKGROUND"!! The coolest part?! If for ANY reason location were to become unavailable, Medialab can use your IP address, or WiFi SSID info.
They will even keep tabs on your access times, pages viewed, user and content interactions and "other information" about your interactions with their Services, as well as with other users, hashtags, and groups across their services.
They also keep any eye on your battery and signal strength, "unique device identifiers" and mobile network information.
Dude, honestly, just fucking read this one in it's entirety. Medialab may be trying harder to protect your information that may be considers sensitive than any other company I've ever done a deep dive into.
They are also dedicated to ensuring that you get the perfect advertisements to match your mood for the moment. They can help you satisfy your specific cravings before you've even begun to crave them.
They only share your information with a small handful of third-parties.
They're quite modest if you ask me.
You can sleep comfortably knowing your information is safe in the trusted hands of Medialab, and its current and future parents, affiliates, subsidiaries, and other companies under common control and ownership, as permitted by applicable law, of course. They may also share information about you among the various Services in order to provide YOU a more consistent and optimal experience!! And also so they can enforce their policies.
#bottomshelfmemes
While using their services, you may be presented with content and links to an advertiser, other third parties, as well as Medialab, of course. They are NOT responsible for how an advertiser and such other third parties collect or use your information. Whatever happens between your information and those third parties is between them and god.
Medialab encourages you to read the privacy policies of EVERY advertiser and third party service that you visit or use.
Although they try their best, life ain't perfect. Unfortunately, they cannot warrant or guarantee that your information will remain secure. They also can't guarantee that your information will not be accessed, disclosed, altered, destroyed, or used in an unauthorized manner. "These are the risks of being online and conducting business online." Hell, if they learn of a security breach, they may even attempt to notify you!!
They make sure it is EZPZ for you to disable online tracking, because they care about your privacy.
Click here --> https://medialab.la/contact if you would like to join Medialab as they consume the internet and let them know what you think of them!
One of their offices is even IN THE FUCKIN BEACH!! Medialab FTW!!
Deaf cat Magoo (short for Oogala Magoogala) tax https://bsky.app/profile/bottomshelfvodka.bsky.social/post/3lxekfqhh6k25
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.title: Fuck you Utah GOP
item.description: https://www.ksl.com/article/51365467/judge-orders-legislature-to-redraw-utahs-congressional-maps-before-next-years-midterms Utah passed a citizens referendum to give the power to draw maps to an independent commission. Utah legislature decided to rewrite the bill that was passed by the citizens to make the independent commission an advisory-only board, where final decision for maps remains with the legislature. Utah citizens have a long history of being overruled by the supermajority who rule the state. (The state even gave themselves the power to alter citizen referendums after they pass, essentially stealing the power back) But the State Supreme Court in a surprising move told the legislature they overstepped and attempted to (did) take our (citizens) state constitutionally granted right to alter our government as we see fit. The supreme court kicked the case back down to a lower court to decide what should be done after finding the state stole our rights, and today that court told the state they have 30 days to draw a new map or to supply a plan of action that will ensure a new map is written before next years midterms. Utah had a competitive district before these maps. We even had a dem representative from time to time, but the new maps cut Salt Lake County between all 4 of our house districts, which made them all skew heavily republican. It's possible this move could give us another chance at a house seat aside from what is happening in Texas and California. Tl;dr: fuck you Utah gop. P.s. obligatory: fuck you medialabs

item.title: Just in case Imgur implodes…
item.description: So @TinyOctopus helped me create a Bluesky and a YouTube account a few months ago when someone was pretending to be me. That was very smart, especially since now it seems like Imgur is going under. I REALLY hope I am wrong, but if it does happen, I want you all to know where to find me: Here’s my Bluesky account: https://bsky.app/profile/kittensfordays.bsky.social/post/3lxdobantcc2f And here’s my YouTube account: https://youtube.com/@therealkittensfordays I am going to do my absolute best to be a prolific poster of videos on YouTube and pictures on Bluesky. You guys are just as invested in these kittens as I am. But fingers crossed, we can keep Imgur alive.
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: Big Trouble In Little China - The most 80's Anti-80's movie.
item.description:
Alright, this is for the folks who voted on my lil poll in @Elbowdeepinahorse discord server. Right from the start I gotta warn you that this is one of my favorite movies, so I will be a bit biased, although I will try my best to be fair in my scrutiny. Big Trouble in Little China, one of the biggest bombs in John Carpenter's curriculum due to Fox's sabotage (it was released ONE WEEK from Alien), yet its loved by whole generations. Considered far ahead of its time, a cheesy subversive action movie, is it as good as everyone say? I removed the nostalgia goggles and watched this movie again, so let's find out.
SO WHAT IS IT ABOUT? Originally a western movie, Big Trouble in Little China is an action-fantasy movie directed by the one and only John Carpenter. Released in 1986, it tells the story of Jack Burton (Kurt Russel) a trucker with a penchant to tell his stories as he traves on the Porkchop Express. One day, while delivering cargo to San Francisco's Chinatown, he meets his friend Wang Chi (Dennis Dun), a restaurant owner. Things go awry when Wang Chi's fiancée, Miao Yin (Suzee Pai) is kidnapped! At first Jack thinks that this will be a simple matter of gang warfare... except that he is way out of his depth, for the person responsible for the kidnapping is none other than David Lo Pan (James Hong), company owner and all out evil sorcerer!
VISUALS If you have seen any John Carpenter movie, you know that, regardless of the quality of the overall flick, the visuals are striking. Be it camera angles, cuts or just the clothing, he strives for images that will stick into your mind. The fallen statue of liberty is iconic, the dog-creature in 'The Thing' and so on. This movie may lack the iconic shots, but it more than compensates everywhere else. The costumes are diverse and really striking, from thugs of different gangs to Lo Pan himself and his three Storms. Their outfits and style were so damn cool they inspired Mortal Kombat! Scenarios are quite good as well, from the shabby streets of San Francisco, to the dusty underground of a building, sewers and the lavish offices of a supernatural CEO. Said backgrounds are colorful, striking and really well done, with things jumping the shark with neon-covered demon statues by the end, something worth of a Masters of the Universe movie! While the directing knows that the movie is meant to be absurd, it balances well between the ludicrous and the realistic, and in a nice subversion of his movies of the time, Big Trouble in Little China is quite bright.
The majority of the special effects and chroma key still holds up very well, especially nowadays contrasting with modern CGI. Anything related to Lightning, the Storm that inspired Raiden is still cool as hell, like seeing him blowing up stuff with some wiggle of his arms, or riding his lightning as if he was Mary Poppins. The monsters such as the ogre are still disgusting and scary, especially considering that normally practical costumes or chroma key are easy to 'sour' to modern eyes. Sadly, not everything held up, especially with the advents of clearer images. Whenever someone beats up a doll is clearer than ever and some of action scenes got... cheesy, and not in a good way. Some silly aspects that would be chalked up as minor problems suddenly become huge eyesores. The camera itself... its John Carpenter going full action. Its good with its limitations. He knows how to put up a good pacing, with dialogues are used to properly raise or lower a tension, quick cuts being followed by longer cuts and so on, but here you see some of the tidbits of styles long lost, like the 'corridor shot' mixed with an angle from below to show that a character is a threat, or pauses to establish comedic moments, with a longer cut going back and forth between characters to let a comedic scene boil before reaching its apex. So it was quite good but nothing overly superb for its time... but for nowadays? It aged like wine... at least for most part.
ACTING This one will be also quite fast and easy. Yeah the acting is good. This is Kurt Russel at the middle of his career, when it was still picking up steam after a string of problems. He feared that this movie would be another bomb in his curriculum (which sadly ended true), but Carpenter told him that... it didn't matter. This seems to be the overall impression of Russel's acting here, its good and he nails the complexities of the character, but he doesn't go overboard and feels as if he is having fun with his role, because success doesn't matter. This was my first movie seeing Dennis Dun in action, and I think that he did quite a fine job, he does have a nice chemistry with Kurt Russel, nothing really bombastic but good. You do have the idea that both are friends and that Wang enjoys Jack's company especially due to all the bravado, feeling as if he is using Jack to lift his own spirits. Sadly whenever its time to get more serious and add some gravitas to the situation, he struggles, but again this is an action movie, not Shakespeare.
The actors of the three storms also struggle to act, because they are martial artists first, but Carpenter wisely keeps their acting scenes to a minimum, reserving them to when the action starts. On the other hand, Lo Pan is one of, if not the most iconic role of James Hong in his really long career, and its for a damn good reason. He easily dances the line between the cheesiness of an action movie and the creepiness of a horror creature with gusto, and is quite expressive both in face and behavior regardless of the layers of makeup on him. Of the support cast, I have no complaints, Kim Cattrall does play a good electric lawyer focused on the truth, which synergizes well with Kurt Russel's more showboating 'smooth' man. Victor Wong as Egg Shen is just as iconic as Lo Pan, serving very, very well as a counter balance between the weird and the mundane, also seemingly having a lot of fun in his role. Sadly, not everything aged gracefully. Ironically, the action scenes are by far the biggest weakness of the movie. For the time the movie was made, they were actually quite good and John Carpenter does quick cuts well to hide the stunts and make a chaotic feeling while keeping it visible enough. But nowadays, after an entire generation of Jackie Chan movies with full contact fights, seeing this movie again made me realize how insanely stiff everyone was. The ideas for the scenes are good, and so are the tricks of the trade such as reverse cameras, trampolines in explosions... but against the more developed techniques introduced by the Hong Kong cinema, they seem insanely simplistic and downright outdated. The air joust scene is still cool as fuuuuuck though.
WRITING While this is an action movie spoofing the eighties action hero style, seeing Big Trouble In Little China again made me realize how stupidly nuanced everything was. Pretty much like Sinners, this is a movie with a straightforward premise. You have the hero, you have the sidekick, the sidekick's fiancée is kidnapped, and they storm the castle. Then you start paying attention and realize that Jack Burton is, in fact, the sidekick, Wang is the main character, Jack is 'just' the protagonist and audience surrogate, a stranger in a strange land. And he is quite well-written too! While he is a spoof of the muscled action hero of the time, he is quite nuanced in several ways. For example he is the kind of 'jerk with a heart of gold' that I admire, because for all his bravado, the very second Wang needs any sort of help, he immediately is there to offer his hand. He starts to hesitate only when things turn supernatural and he realizes that he is way over his head, but even then there are several moments where he shows empathy, no matter how much he says that he only wants his truck. He is comedically ineffectual in combat because he is just a brawler in a world of martial arts with constant death, but he is quick on his thinking and the movie establishes his quick reflexes for a reason and won't stop showboating and behaving as if he was a self-aware Rambo. Though I have to admit that he ends a bit TOO ineffectual, with some moments ending a bit too ridiculous, and not in a good way, taking time from the awesome combat.
And THEN, after you watched Jack Burton be the front of the movie, you gently nudge him to the side and realizes that, while this is still a straightforward modern-fantasy movie, there is a lot of nuances and show-don't-tell that modern audiences aren't used to. There isn't much depth to the characters themselves besides Jack, Wang is the usual action protagonist worried about his lost love, Lo Pan is the hammy evil sorcerer... but take a look and how the gears move and you'll see a whole different plane, for example, its never stated how Wang learned how to fight, but we just accept that he does since he is Chinese, which I consider that Carpenter is using the old archetype 'Every chinese knows kung fu' in the movie's favor... and even then subverting it because Wang's host Eddie doesn't. But its still crystal clear that both are so used to death, they killed before. There are lots of underlying themes of a Chinese population ingrained with USA culture without losing much, mostly adding. Egg Shen talks about the transcontinental roads and how Chinatown came to be, one of Lo Pan's underlings use Old West style revolvers with the quick-draw pose and Lo Pan used the American Dream to bring his empire into fruition. One may argue that the Chinese community was the one that brought Lo Pan into America, as if there was a piece of their homeland coexisting with this new world. Egg Shen's last words are how China is within his heart, so there is no need to leave America.
And in this mixed heritage story, there are several details shown, not told, that need a good eye and mind to catch. For example Gracie Law, the lawyer, seems to have Chinese descendancy: she understands everyone, everyone knows her (especially hate her, finding her annoying), and her surname has a double-meaning. Its also never stated, but Lo Pan needed a Chinese woman of green eyes, something probably impossible, unless there was mixed heritage, and he also seizes Gracie. For a fantasy action movie, there is a lot to read! But sadly, nothing is perfect and there were some big problems in our little China. The first time I watched this movie, was the theatrical version... and lo and behold, I was surprised when the version I got had a weird, needless intro with Egg Shen talking with a lawyer. This intro serves to nothing but spoil that magic is real and could be easily cut, so I wasn't surprised to discover it was an addition mandated by producers. While the pacing of the movie is good, with ups and downs, action scenes being interceded with calmer scenes and comedy spruced up, after rewatching this flick I realized that the whole prostibule part of the movie is just filler that could be easily cut. While comedy is spruced here and there like garlic on Italian pasta keeping a good balance with the action and horror, sometimes it gets too much leading to frustration, for example the Storms are played as dreadful enemies to fight, night unstoppable... and each one of their deaths is hilarious but at the same time heavily anticlimactic.
And this will be up for each one's interpretation, but Miao Yin is ye classic princess Peach in another castle. She barely features in the movie and its there to be saved. Honestly? I don't mind it. Could have been used better, but it's the usual fare of the time. She contrasts nicely with Gracie, who is far more active, and its a shame they didn't interact at all. Even then, the story is simple, balancing horror, action and humor very well, helped by the dialogues. It doesn't try to bite more than it can chew, although it did have space to do so. It knows its cheesy, but it also knows to take itself seriously enough to immerse the viewer in. I can parody elements of the movies of the time without being a farse, or worse, acerbic, qualities that are sorely missed nowadays.
SOUNDTRACK Ok. This one is genuinely weird. I was surprised to discover that John Carpenter had a hand in the soundtrack, nothing bad and all and when I rewatched this movie, I found the music to be quite fitting. The synthesizers are well used, the rhythm follows what happens in the screen quite well, elevating or diminishing the tension. But the weirdness kicked in when I heard the soundtrack by itself. Its not bad but... its definitely chaotic! While there is an obvious rhythm, at times it feels as if Carpenter started just throwing instruments in to see what would stick. At times there is a marimba in the middle! The soundtrack would definitely have benefited from someone to clean up the extras. Other than that, the sound effects aren't bad. Definitely cheesy, and it may be up to personal tastes, but to me they helped settle in the absurdity of seeing two Chinese just flying through the air clashing swords with a weird wooosh! Final Thoughts Again, I love this movie, and thus you should take what I say here with a grain of salt. But after going through several successes of the past without the nostalgia goggles, even movies that I loved as a kid, I feel that Big Trouble in Little China is simply really good. It is cheesy, but it balances well with the action, it doesn't take itself seriously but doesn't badmouth its own premise or treat everything in it with scorn. No wonder that, even if it was sabotaged by Fox by being launched one week before Alien of all movies, it endured and became a fan favorite. It has its shortcomings, but they don't stop the fun I had. RECOMMEND? ABSO-FUCKING-LUTELY. Thank you for reading this far! I do these reviews as a hobby, so I'm always open for feedback and suggestions. This week I've reviewed the one and only SUPERMAN by James Gunn! Does it hold up to scrutiny of someone immune to hype? Check it out at https://youtu.be/YI8SI9Nkes4 If you're interested in checking my other reviews, visit my site. https://hypeimmune.wordpress.com/ #hypeimmune Oh and lastly FOCK DA BUSEHNESS DADDEH!
item.dc:creator: Skullwriter

item.title: Remember kids, always read the TOS.
item.description: #bottomshelfmemes This is just a clip. Here's a link to the whole thing https://imgur.com/gallery/qo63Eyu
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.title: Found a tool to save your favorites in case Business Daddy decides to dump imgur as a whole
item.description:
Step 1: Install python 3.8 or higher Step 2: in the terminal use "python3 -m pip install -U gallery-dl" Step 3: still in terminal use "gallery-dl https://imgur.com/user/[USERNAME]/favorites" This will create a folder called gallery-dl at the location the terminal is at, with a folder for each post with the images/videos/etc in them (as seen above) While i hope it doesn't come to it, better to have all your favorites than to lose them when medialab flips their metaphorical table and shuts it all down. PS: locked folders need unlocked during your download. Tool's src: https://github.com/mikf/gallery-dl
Front Page Edit: First off, thank you all. Secondly, if anyone wants to join a life raft me and many others have joined this discord: https://discord.gg/XyXz7dPj The community is our strength, stick together though these troubled waters.
item.dc:creator: etopsirhc
